words.definition: the ability to maintain something over time without damaging the environment
example_sentences: Sustainability is now central to all business decisions.
She studies sustainability at university.
We need to move towards greater sustainability in food production.
words.pronunciation_tip: sus-tayn-uh-BIL-ih-tee — six syllables, stress on fourth.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'being good for the environment' only — 'sustainability' is broader.

words.definition: the amount of CO2 produced by a person's or organisation's activities
example_sentences: Flying frequently increases your carbon footprint.
The company has pledged to reduce its carbon footprint by 50%.
You can offset your carbon footprint by planting trees.
words.pronunciation_tip: KAR-bun FOOT-print.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'pollution I cause' — say 'my carbon footprint'.
words.collocations: ['reduce carbon footprint', 'calculate carbon footprint', 'carbon footprint offset']

words.definition: energy from sources that are naturally replenished, like wind or sun
example_sentences: The country is investing heavily in renewable energy.
Renewable energy is cheaper than it was ten years ago.
Solar and wind are the most common forms of renewable energy.
words.pronunciation_tip: rih-NYOO-uh-bul EN-er-jee.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'clean energy' and 'renewable energy' interchangeably — they overlap but aren't identical.
words.collocations: ['invest in renewable energy', 'switch to renewable energy', 'renewable energy source']

words.definition: the cutting down of forests on a large scale
example_sentences: Deforestation in the Amazon is destroying biodiversity.
Deforestation contributes significantly to climate change.
Stopping deforestation is essential for the planet's health.
words.pronunciation_tip: dee-for-ih-STAY-shun — five syllables, stress on fourth.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'cutting trees' — say 'deforestation' for large-scale clearing.

words.definition: the variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat
example_sentences: Tropical rainforests contain enormous biodiversity.
Biodiversity is declining at an alarming rate.
Protecting biodiversity is essential for ecosystem health.
words.pronunciation_tip: by-oh-dy-VUR-sih-tee — six syllables, stress on fourth.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'different types of animals and plants' every time — say 'biodiversity'.

words.definition: gases released into the atmosphere, especially CO2
example_sentences: The country has pledged to cut emissions by 2030.
Car emissions contribute significantly to air pollution.
They measured the factory's emissions carefully.
words.pronunciation_tip: ih-MISH-unz — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'pollution gases' — say 'emissions' in environmental contexts.

words.definition: a long period with very little rain causing water shortage
example_sentences: The region suffered its worst drought in fifty years.
Drought threatens food security in many countries.
Drought conditions have become more frequent with climate change.
words.pronunciation_tip: DROWT — rhymes with 'out'. One syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'no rain for a long time' — say 'drought'.

words.definition: all the living things in an area and their relationship with each other
example_sentences: The ocean ecosystem is incredibly complex.
Plastic pollution is damaging marine ecosystems.
Human activity is disrupting natural ecosystems worldwide.
words.pronunciation_tip: EE-koh-sis-tum — four syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'nature environment' — say 'ecosystem'.

words.definition: to make something impure or harmful by introducing pollutants
example_sentences: Industrial waste contaminated the local river.
The water supply was contaminated with chemicals.
Contaminated soil can remain toxic for decades.
words.pronunciation_tip: kun-TAM-ih-nayt — four syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'make dirty' in environmental contexts — say 'contaminate'.

words.definition: the natural environment where an animal or plant lives
example_sentences: Polar bears are losing their natural habitat.
Deforestation destroys the habitat of thousands of species.
Urban growth encroaches on natural habitats.
words.pronunciation_tip: HAB-ih-tat — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'where animals live' every time — say 'habitat'.

words.definition: at risk of dying out completely
example_sentences: The tiger is an endangered species.
Climate change is making more species endangered.
Many endangered animals are protected by international law.
words.pronunciation_tip: en-DAYN-jerd — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'nearly extinct' — say 'endangered'. Extinct = already gone. Endangered = at risk.

words.definition: a large area of land where waste is buried
example_sentences: Most household waste ends up in landfill.
Reducing landfill is a key environmental goal.
Plastic in landfill takes hundreds of years to break down.
words.pronunciation_tip: LAND-fil — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'rubbish dump' in formal contexts — say 'landfill'.

words.definition: to process used materials so they can be used again
example_sentences: Always recycle paper, glass, and plastic.
The factory recycles 90% of its production waste.
Recycling reduces the amount that goes to landfill.
words.pronunciation_tip: ree-SY-kul — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'use again' — 'recycle' specifically means processing for reuse.

words.definition: a device that converts sunlight into electricity
example_sentences: They installed solar panels on the roof last year.
Solar panels can power an entire household.
The cost of solar panels has fallen dramatically.
words.pronunciation_tip: SOH-ler PAN-ul.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'sun electricity machine' — say 'solar panel'.
words.collocations: ['install solar panels', 'solar panel system', 'solar panel energy']

words.definition: an overflow of water onto normally dry land
example_sentences: The village was devastated by the worst flood in living memory.
Climate change is making floods more frequent.
Heavy rain flooded the underground car park.
words.pronunciation_tip: FLUD — one syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the water came over the land' — say 'there was a flood'.

words.definition: to compensate for something negative, such as carbon emissions
example_sentences: The airline offers passengers the chance to offset their emissions.
They offset carbon by investing in reforestation projects.
Carbon offsetting is controversial but widely used.
words.pronunciation_tip: OFF-set — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'compensate for my pollution' — say 'offset my carbon footprint'.

words.definition: the protection and careful management of the environment
example_sentences: Wildlife conservation is vital for future generations.
She works for a marine conservation organisation.
Conservation efforts have saved several species from extinction.
words.pronunciation_tip: kon-ser-VAY-shun — four syllables, stress on third.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'protecting nature' as a noun — say 'conservation'.

words.definition: the gradual wearing away of land or rock by water or wind
example_sentences: Coastal erosion is threatening several seaside towns.
Soil erosion leads to loss of fertile farmland.
Tree roots help to prevent soil erosion.
words.pronunciation_tip: ih-ROH-zhun — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the land is washed away' as a noun — say 'erosion'.

words.definition: the gradual increase in the Earth's average temperature
example_sentences: Global warming is the result of greenhouse gas emissions.
The effects of global warming include rising sea levels.
Limiting global warming to 1.5°C is the key target.
words.pronunciation_tip: GLOH-bul WAWM-ing.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the world is getting hot' — say 'global warming'. The formal term is 'climate change'.
words.collocations: ['cause global warming', 'effects of global warming', 'limit global warming']

words.definition: producing no net carbon dioxide emissions
example_sentences: The company has committed to being zero-carbon by 2040.
Zero-carbon buildings use no fossil fuels.
Achieving a zero-carbon economy will take decades.
words.pronunciation_tip: ZEER-oh KAR-bun.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'no pollution' — say 'zero-carbon' for no CO2 emissions.
